context: Tests whether the agent applies the write-a-skill review checklist to identify and fix problems in an existing SKILL.md: missing 'Use when' triggers, time-sensitive information, poor description quality, and lack of concrete examples. The fixture SKILL.md has all four problems deliberately embedded.

checklist.name: Use when added to description
checklist.description: The improved description includes a 'Use when' clause with specific triggers (keywords or contexts that should cause the skill to load)

checklist.name: Time-sensitive info removed
checklist.description: The improved SKILL.md does NOT contain any time-sensitive information (no specific dates, no 'as of Q1 2026', no 'moving to Teams in Q2', no date-stamped template versions)

checklist.name: Third-person description
checklist.description: The improved frontmatter description is written in third person (no 'I will', 'I'll', 'we use')

checklist.name: Concrete example included
checklist.description: The improved SKILL.md includes at least one concrete, realistic example (a sample meeting note excerpt, a worked action item, or a filled-in template)

checklist.name: SKILL.md under 100 lines
checklist.description: The improved SKILL.md is 100 lines or fewer

checklist.name: Internal-only references removed
checklist.description: The improved SKILL.md does NOT reference internal-only resources (no wiki.internal links, no VPN references, no internal tool names that would be inaccessible to an external agent)

checklist.name: Description specificity improved
checklist.description: The improved description is more specific than 'Helps with meeting notes. Good for meetings.' — it explains the capability and when to use it meaningfully

checklist.name: Review report created
checklist.description: A review-report.md file exists and lists at least two specific problems found in the original

checklist.name: Core purpose preserved
checklist.description: The improved skill is still about meeting notes (not rewritten for a completely different domain)

checklist.name: Consistent terminology
checklist.description: The improved SKILL.md uses consistent terminology throughout — the same word for the same concept each time it appears
